model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Despite being the 9-seed, Iowa is the statistically superior team, ranking 25th in KenPom compared to Clemson's 36th, with a meaningful +3.15 adjusted efficiency margin advantage driven by a significantly better offense (AdjO 121.8 vs 116.6, a roughly 5-point gap per 100 possessions). While Clemson counters with an elite defense ranked 20th nationally in Torvik (AdjDE 96.9 vs Iowa's 100.2), Iowa's offensive advantage is roughly 2.5 times larger than Clemson's defensive edge, and the Hawkeyes' Big Ten schedule provides comparable battle-testing to Clemson's ACC slate. In a classic 8/9 coin-flip where the metrics favor the lower seed, Iowa's superior overall efficiency profile and offensive firepower give them a narrow but real edge.

reasoningStep.title: South 8/9: Clemson vs Iowa
reasoningStep.summary: Iowa's higher KenPom ranking (#25 vs #36) and significantly better offense (AdjO 121.8 vs 116.6) outweigh Clemson's defensive advantage (AdjD 97.3 vs 99.4), giving the Hawkeyes a narrow edge in this 8/9 matchup despite being the lower seed.
evidence: KenPom: Iowa #25 (AdjEM +22.41, AdjO 121.8/31st, AdjD 99.4) vs Clemson #36 (AdjEM +19.26, AdjO 116.6/71st, AdjD 97.3)
Torvik: Iowa #26 (AdjOE 122.1/29th, AdjDE 100.2/35th) vs Clemson #29 (AdjOE 116.5/69th, AdjDE 96.9/20th)
Iowa's ~5-point offensive efficiency advantage per 100 possessions significantly exceeds Clemson's ~2-point defensive edge
Both teams from power conferences: Iowa (Big Ten, 21-12) vs Clemson (ACC, 24-10)
Classic 8/9 coin-flip where metrics favor the lower-seeded Iowa

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

Now let me compile my analysis based on the available data.

**Key Statistical Comparison:**

| Metric | Clemson (8-seed) | Iowa (9-seed) |
|--------|-----------------|---------------|
| KenPom Rank | 36 | 25 |
| Torvik Rank | 29 | 26 |
| Record | 24-10 (ACC) | 21-12 (Big Ten) |
| AdjO (KenPom) | 116.6 (71st) | 121.8 (31st) |
| AdjD (KenPom) | 97.3 | 99.4 |
| AdjEM (KenPom) | +19.26 | +22.41 |
| AdjOE (Torvik) | 116.5 (69th) | 122.1 (29th) |
| AdjDE (Torvik) | 96.9 (20th) | 100.2 (35th) |

**Analysis:**
- Iowa has a **+3.15 point** efficiency margin advantage in KenPom and is ranked 11 spots higher
- Iowa's offense is significantly better: 121.8 vs 116.6 AdjO (~5 points per 100 possessions)
- Clemson's defense is better: 97.3 vs 99.4 AdjD (~2 points per 100 possessions)
- Iowa's offensive advantage (~5 pts) outweighs Clemson's defensive advantage (~2 pts)
- Both played in power conferences (ACC vs Big Ten)
- Clemson has the better record (24-10 vs 21-12), but Iowa played in the arguably tougher Big Ten
- Despite being the 9-seed, Iowa is the statistically superior team

This is a classic 8/9 coin-flip, but Iowa has a meaningful edge in the metrics.
